level_signals: 4-6 years of experience in US immigration required
Role title is Senior Immigration Program Manager
Base comp range $105,400–$155,000 consistent with Senior IC
Reports into Global Immigration Manager (not a manager-of-managers role)

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have at least 4-6 years of experience in US immigration (e.g., at a law firm, vendor, or in-house program) within a high-volume environment.
You've supported or managed high-volume immigration programs and thrive on taking ownership of day-to-day execution with accuracy and reliability.
You have deep expertise in common US nonimmigrant visa categories (H-1B, L-1, TN, E-3, O-1), as well as experience managing large PERM and immigrant visa programs.
You stay up to date with the latest changes in the US immigration landscape to identify potential areas of risk and plan mitigation strategies.
You're able to manage multiple cases at once with accuracy and care, and you communicate clearly and collaboratively with employees and partners
You thrive in a fast-paced, changing environment and are eager to grow your scope over time.
You have experience with case management systems or centralized vendor platforms to manage immigration cases efficiently.
You're a collaborative team player who enjoys working cross-functionally and continuously improving how immigration programs operate.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You're curious and open to thoughtfully using AI tools to enhance efficiency, insights, and impact in your work.
You'll own and drive the execution of our US immigration program, with opportunities to expand into the wider Americas region over time.

scope.surface: US immigration program within Global Mobility & Immigration (GMI) team; potential expansion to Americas region
scope.business_model: technology and logistics / marketplace (gig economy, 40+ country operations)
primary_metrics_inferred: Timely and compliant immigration case processing
Data accuracy and reporting across immigration portfolio
Employee and manager satisfaction with immigration support
core_responsibilities: Managing day-to-day immigration cases (work visas, green cards, compliance tracking) in close partnership with employees, managers, recruiters, and vendors
Act as a subject matter expert for complex immigration scenarios and serve as the internal point of contact
Support process improvements, data accuracy, and reporting across our immigration portfolio
Contribute to scalable practices as our team grows, proactively identifying program gaps and opportunities for improvement
Partner with cross-functional teams (Legal, Talent Acquisition) and our larger GMI team to ensure alignment and compliance

green_flags: Comprehensive benefits package including 16 weeks paid parental leave
Clear reporting structure stated (reports to Global Immigration Manager)
40+ country operations suggests meaningful scope and growth potential
Explicit statement of non-discrimination and diversity commitment
red_flags: Work location type not specified (onsite, hybrid, or remote not stated) — unclear for candidates planning around commute or relocation
Visa sponsorship for this role not explicitly addressed in JD
Years range '4-6 years' is ambiguous as a floor — could be read as requiring 6 years by some hiring managers

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from role title, comp range, and reporting structure)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated in JD)
location.type (not stated in JD body — left as 'unclear')
ambiguities: Years of experience stated as '4-6 years' — this phrasing is unusual (typically a minimum is given, not a band); treated as minimum 4 years with 6 as the preferred target
Work location not specified anywhere in the JD body; pay is described as localized by work location, implying multiple geographies are possible, but whether remote, hybrid, or onsite is not stated
The Americas expansion mention is framed as an opportunity ('over time'), not a current requirement — classified as nice-to-have
